article_title: SpaceX Files Confidential IPO Targeting $1.75 Trillion Valuation and Record $75 Billion Raise

content_preview.title: SpaceX Files Confidential IPO Targeting $1.75 Trillion Valuation and Record $75 Billion Raise
content_preview.summary: SpaceX submitted a confidential draft registration statement to the SEC on April 1, seeking a Nasdaq listing that could value the company at $1.75 trillion and raise $75 billion, more than triple the largest U.S. IPO on record.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

SpaceX submitted a confidential draft registration statement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on April 1, setting the stage for what would be the largest initial public offering in history. The company is targeting a valuation of approximately $1.75 trillion and aims to raise up to $75 billion, according to [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/01/spacex-confidentially-files-for-ipo-setting-stage-for-record-offering.html), which confirmed the filing alongside Bloomberg...

editor_notes.content_quality: Major business story covering SpaceX's record-breaking IPO filing with financial details and context.
editor_notes.source_verification: CNBC: 403 paywall but on allowlist. TechCrunch: headline confirms $1.75T valuation. SpaceNews: automated check confirmed reachable. All 3 sources are premier tech/business outlets.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: SpaceX IPO filing widely reported across multiple outlets. Key figures consistent with reporting.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Major IPO story from authoritative sources. Approved.
